Why Rook exists

Built to make AI less intimidating.

Rook is designed for people who are curious about AI but do not want to start with a terminal, confusing install steps, or a pile of new terms. Rook gives you a cleaner starting point, then helps you grow into more powerful AI workflows as you learn.

A quick look at the mission

Rook helps beginners step into AI with less friction.

This visual sums up the direction behind Rook: a cleaner, more welcoming starting point for people who want to get into AI without first becoming command-line experts.

Core features

A simple start, with room to grow.

Five helpful modes

Use Fast, Standard, Critical Thinking, Coder, and Raw modes as simple lanes for different kinds of AI work.

Local and cloud options

Start with Ollama models and choose local or supported cloud-connected models depending on the task.

Project workspace

Keep notes, context, and progress connected to the thing you are actually working on.

Remember useful details

Save hardware notes, preferences, project facts, and important details so Rook can stay more useful over time.

Keep your conversations

Return to previous chats and continue learning instead of starting over every time.

Attach images and text

Send images to vision-capable models or attach large pasted text without making the chat messy.

Easy onboarding

Download Rook first. Let it point you in the right direction.

Rook is built for people who may not know what Ollama, LLMs, APIs, or command-line tools are yet. If Ollama is not already installed, the Rook installer opens the Ollama download page during setup so the path is easier to follow.

1
Download Rook
Grab the installer and launch setup like a normal Windows app.
2
Installer checks the basics
If you do not already have Ollama, the installer opens the Ollama download page to help you get it installed.
3
Pick your model and start
Connect Rook to your Ollama models, assign them to brains, and begin working.

Before installing

System requirements

Required

  • Windows computer
  • Ollama installed and running
  • At least one compatible Ollama model

Recommended

  • Modern multi-core CPU
  • 16 GB RAM or more
  • NVIDIA GPU for stronger local AI performance
  • 12 GB+ VRAM preferred for larger models

Good to know

  • Rook is the interface, not the AI model itself
  • Performance depends on your hardware and model choice
  • Private work is best kept on local models

Questions

FAQ

Does Rook include AI models?

No. Rook is the friendly desktop interface. It connects to models through Ollama and helps make the experience easier to start using.

Does Rook support cloud models?

Yes. Rook supports local Ollama models and supported cloud-connected Ollama models, so you can choose the right fit for the task.

Do users need to install Ollama first?

No. You can download Rook first. If Ollama is not already installed, the Rook installer opens the Ollama download page during setup to help you get started.

Is Rook beginner-friendly?

Yes. Rook is built for people who are curious about AI but do not want to start by learning command prompts, APIs, or technical model setup. Once you have a model running, you can ask Rook to explain AI concepts from inside Rook itself.

What does “choose your price” mean?

The V1 release is meant to work with a pay-what-you-want style payment flow, with a low entry point and the option for supporters to pay more.

Is this finished commercial software?

V1 should be presented honestly as an early public release or beta: usable, polished, and real, but still early in the product’s life.
